corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: Imagen achieves state of the art zero-shot FID on COCO at 7.27, outperforming the concurrent work of DALL-E 2 and even models trained on COCO.
Imagen achieves state of the art zero-shot FID on COCO at 7.27, outperforming the concurrent work of DALL-E 2 and even models trained on COCO.
For photorealism, Imagen achieves 39.2% preference rate indicating high image quality generation.

We observe that conditioning over the sequence of text embeddings with cross attention significantly outperforms simple mean or attention based pooling in both sample fidelity as well as image-text alignment.
In future work we will explore a framework for responsible externalization that balances the value of external auditing with the risks of unrestricted open-access.
Another ethical challenge relates to the large scale data requirements of text-to-image models, which have have led researchers to rely heavily on large, mostly uncurated, web-scraped datasets.

While we leave an in-depth empirical analysis of social and cultural biases encoded by Imagen to future work, our small scale internal assessments reveal several limitations that guide our decision not to release Imagen at this time.

We believe this is a critical avenue for future research and we intend to explore benchmark evaluations for social and cultural bias in future work-for example, exploring whether it is possible to generalize the normalized pointwise mutual information metric to the measurement of biases in image generation models.
We aim to address these challenges in future work.

A diffusion model xθ is trained on a denoising objective of the form where (x, c) are data-conditioning pairs, t ∼ U([0, 1]), ∼ N (0, I), and α t , σ t , w t are functions of t that influence sample quality.
Base model: We adapt the U-Net architecture from for our base 64 × 64 text-to-image diffusion model.
With similar motivation, we introduce DrawBench, a comprehensive and challenging set of prompts that support the evaluation and comparison of text-to-image models.
We present Imagen, a text-to-image diffusion model with an unprecedented degree of photorealism and a deep level of language understanding.

Imagen achieves a new state-of-the-art FID score of 7.27 on the COCO dataset, without ever training on COCO, and human raters find Imagen samples to be on par with the COCO data itself in image-text alignment.

Imagen achieves a new state-of-the-art FID score of 7.27 on the COCO dataset, without ever training on COCO, and human raters find Imagen samples to be on par with the COCO data itself in image-text alignment.

Imagen achieves a new state-of-the-art FID score of 7.27 on the COCO dataset, without ever training on COCO, and human raters find Imagen samples to be on par with the COCO data itself in image-text alignment.
To assess text-to-image models in greater depth, we introduce DrawBench, a comprehensive and challenging benchmark for text-to-image models.
To assess text-to-image models in greater depth, we introduce DrawBench, a comprehensive and challenging benchmark for text-to-image models.

To pursue this research direction further, we introduce Imagen, a text-to-image diffusion model that combines the power of transformer language models (LMs) with high-fidelity diffusion models to deliver an unprecedented degree of photorealism and a deep level of language understanding in text-to-image synthesis.

Autoregressive models , GANs , VQ-VAE Transformer-based methods , and diffusion models have seen remarkable progress in text-to-image , including the concurrent DALL-E 2 , which uses a diffusion prior on CLIP text latents and cascaded diffusion models to generate high resolution 1024 × 1024 images; we believe Imagen is much simpler, as Imagen does not need to learn a latent prior, yet achieves better results in both MS-COCO FID and human evaluation on DrawBench.

Furthermore, making the super-resolution models aware of the amount of noise added, via noise level conditioning, significantly improves the sample quality and helps improving the robustness of the super-resolution models to handle artifacts generated by lower resolution models .
The COCO validation set is the standard benchmark for evaluating text-to-image models for both the supervised and the zero-shot setting .
DrawBench: While COCO is a valuable benchmark, it is increasingly clear that it has a limited spectrum of prompts that do not readily provide insight into differences between models (e.g., see Sec.

Across these 11 categories, DrawBench comprises 200 prompts in total, striking a good balance between the desire for a large, comprehensive dataset, and small enough that human evaluation remains feasible. (Appendix C provides a more detailed description of DrawBench.
Imagen achieves state of the art zero-shot FID on COCO at 7.27, outperforming the concurrent work of DALL-E 2 and even models trained on COCO.

downstream_contract.intended_use: Feed this JSON to a later local open-source model to write an overview.
downstream_contract.grounding_rule: The downstream generator should write from evidence_units.location.sentence and context_window, not from unsupported outside knowledge.
downstream_contract.score_warning: Scores are ranking and filtering signals, not calibrated probabilities.
